step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture from left to right. It starts with the Client Lambda function sending logs via CloudWatch subscription to the first Kinesis stream. A Transform Lambda function consumes this stream, querying and updating a DynamoDB table for customer config and state. This Transform Lambda sends processed logs into a second Kinesis stream. From here, the flow splits into two paths: (1) Fast query path: A Lambda function writes events to Serverless Aurora. (2) Analytical path: Firehose converts JSON to Parquet using Glue's schema and stores it in S3, which is then queried by Athena. A developer actor queries the system for dashboards.

graph.name: Epsagon Serverless Telemetry Processing & Analytics Engine
graph.notes: Handles billions of customer lambda invocations monthly. Uses a split fast-path (Aurora) and analytical-path (Firehose/S3/Athena) pattern. Leverages Kinesis to protect Aurora from connection overloading.

nodes.name: Client Lambda
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Monitored function in customer account running billions of times monthly.

nodes.name: Ingestion Kinesis
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Streams filtered CloudWatch logs from customer account.

nodes.name: Transform Lambda
nodes.notes: Extracts log records into structured invocation records.

nodes.name: Customer Metadata DB
nodes.notes: Stores metadata about customer functions and partial invocation details.

nodes.name: Aurora Writer Lambda
nodes.notes: Writes records to Aurora. Managed concurrency prevents database connection exhaustion.

nodes.name: Serverless Aurora
nodes.notes: Optimized database for fast single-item invocation queries.

nodes.name: Firehose Parquet Converter
nodes.notes: Batches incoming records and converts them to Parquet.

nodes.name: Glue Data Catalog
nodes.notes: Provides structural schema definition for Firehose Parquet conversion.

nodes.name: S3 Data Lake
nodes.notes: Optimized storage bucket for long-term telemetry analysis.

nodes.name: Athena Query Engine
nodes.notes: Performs fast aggregated queries over S3 telemetry.

nodes.name: Epsagon UI Developer
nodes.notes: Represents the user or internal developer querying monitoring metrics.

edges.notes: Sends log data via CloudWatch integration.
